Package [org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators](<package-summary.html>)

# Interface InputSelectable

All Known Implementing Classes:
    `[BatchCoBroadcastWithNonKeyedOperator](<co/BatchCoBroadcastWithNonKeyedOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.co")`, `[BatchMultipleInputStreamOperator](<../../../table/runtime/operators/multipleinput/BatchMultipleInputStreamOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.runtime.operators.multipleinput")`, `[EmbeddedPythonBatchCoBroadcastProcessOperator](<python/embedded/EmbeddedPythonBatchCoBroadcastProcessOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.python.embedded")`, `[ExternalPythonBatchCoBroadcastProcessOperator](<python/process/ExternalPythonBatchCoBroadcastProcessOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.python.process")`, `[FusionStreamOperatorBase](<../../../table/runtime/operators/fusion/FusionStreamOperatorBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.runtime.operators.fusion")`, `[HashJoinOperator](<../../../table/runtime/operators/join/HashJoinOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.runtime.operators.join")`

* * *

[@PublicEvolving](<../../../annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") public interface InputSelectable

Interface for stream operators that can select the input to get [`StreamRecord`](<../../runtime/streamrecord/StreamRecord.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord").

**IMPORTANT:** This interface is a loose contract. The runtime may read multiple records continuously before calling `nextSelection()` again to determine whether to change the input to be read. That is, it is not guaranteed that `nextSelection()` will be called immediately after the operator has processed a record and the reading input will be changed according to [`InputSelection`](<InputSelection.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators") returned. This means that the operator may receive some data that it does not currently want to process. Therefore, if an operator needs a strict convention, it must cache the unexpected data itself and handle them correctly.

This interface also makes the following conventions:

  1. The runtime must call `nextSelection()` to determine the input to read the first record.
  2. When the input being read reaches the end, the runtime must call `nextSelection()` to determine the next input to be read.

`nextSelection()`

  * ## Method Details

    * ### nextSelection

[InputSelection](<InputSelection.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators") nextSelection()

Returns the next [`InputSelection`](<InputSelection.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators") that wants to get the record. This method is guaranteed to not be called concurrently with other methods of the operator.
